idea.hook: Ever wonder how much 'stuff' is truly in our Solar System?
facts: Our Sun accounts for a staggering 99.8% of the Solar System's total mass.
Of the remaining 0.2% (planets, moons, asteroids, comets), Jupiter alone holds 71% of that mass.
This means Jupiter is 2.5 times more massive than all other planets combined, and ~318 times Earth's mass.
idea.payoff: Jupiter isn't just a big planet; it's a colossal, near-star that overwhelmingly dominates almost everything else in our cosmic neighborhood besides the Sun itself.
idea.title: Jupiter: Our Solar System's Mass MONSTER! 🤯🪐

scenes.visual: A visually striking, dark cosmic background with a scattering of distant stars, slowly zooming out slightly to reveal the vastness.

scenes.text: THE SUN: 99.8% of Solar System Mass
scenes.visual: A massive, glowing Sun dominates the screen, with tiny, almost imperceptible dots representing planets orbiting at the very edge. A percentage graphic (99.8%) appears over the Sun.

The huge Sun quickly fades, replaced by a smaller, dynamic graphic representing the 'remaining 0.2%'. Jupiter then grows significantly within this space, taking up 71% of it, with other planets as much smaller elements.

scenes.text:
Jupiter > All other planets (2.5x)
Jupiter = 318x Earth
scenes.visual: A clear visual comparison: A large, detailed Jupiter with a tiny cluster of all other Solar System planets (Saturn, Uranus, Neptune, Earth, Mars, etc.) orbiting around it. Then, a side-by-side comparison of Jupiter next to a minuscule Earth.

An awe-inspiring, full-screen shot of Jupiter, slowly rotating with its iconic stripes and Great Red Spot. It emits a subtle, powerful glow, emphasizing its immense mass and presence, with distant stars in the background.

youtube.title: Jupiter: Our Solar System's Mass MONSTER! 🤯🪐
